source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the primary structural frame of an autonomous payload-delivery machine benchmark intended to collect two payload objects from Zone A, transport them across an obstacle track, and deposit them in Zone B within 60 seconds, explicitly as a multi-part assembly. The entire machine must fit within a 600 mm x 600 mm x 600 mm starting cube at the start line and its total mass must not exceed 10 kg. The structural frame is built from 6061-T6 aluminum tube and plate (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). The assembly consists of the main chassis box frame (bolted tube + plate construction), the front scoop arm that lifts both payloads, the payload scoop fixture at the end of the arm, the drivetrain cross-member carrying axle mounts, and the bolted fastener set connecting members. Primary load cases: (LC1) payload lift: each of two steel payload cubes weighs 1.5 kg; lift both simultaneously from the front scoop arm at a 0.3 m horizontal moment arm forward of the front axle (total moment 8.83 Nm, total load 29.4 N down); (LC2) track obstacle: drive over a 30 mm step at 0.5 m/s, modeled as 3 g vertical impulse on the front axle with base mass 10 kg; (LC3) collision arrest: full machine travelling at 1.0 m/s decelerates from a fixed wall impact in 50 ms (2 m/s^2 = 20 g equivalent) applied to the front of the frame; (LC4) 60-second duty cycle at 1.5 Hz drivetrain oscillation between +/- nominal 29.4 N payload load (90 cycles total). Pass/fail: LC1-LC3 max von Mises stress below 184 MPa (276/1.5), front-arm deflection at 0.3 m arm tip below 5 mm under LC1, scoop motion must not violate the 600 mm starting envelope until after the start-line crossing. First natural frequency of the payload arm must exceed 15 Hz. Verification is on the assembled multi-body model with bolted interfaces; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP model with all bodies, per-case FEA of assembly, modal, and a mass budget spreadsheet breaking out drive, structure, and mechanism mass.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structural frame of an autonomous payload-delivery machine for the Warman-style design-build benchmark, delivered as a multi-part assembly that collects two payloads from Zone A and deposits them in Zone B within 60 seconds.

The machine fits a 600 mm x 600 mm x 600 mm starting cube, total mass <= 10 kg, built from 6061-T6 aluminum tube and plate (E 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3).

Load cases.
- LC1 payload lift: two 1.5 kg steel cubes on a 0.3 m forward moment arm (total 29.4 N, 8.83 N-m).
- LC2 track obstacle: 30 mm step at 0.5 m/s as 3 g vertical impulse on the front axle with 10 kg base mass.
- LC3 collision arrest: 1.0 m/s impact decelerating in 50 ms (20 g equivalent) on the frame front.
- LC4 duty cycle: 1.5 Hz drivetrain oscillation at +/- 29.4 N for 90 cycles over 60 s.

First natural frequency of the payload arm must exceed 15 Hz.

prompt.title: Autonomous payload-delivery robot frame (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part aluminum primary frame for a dual-payload delivery autonomous machine: chassis box, scoop arm, scoop fixture, drivetrain cross-member, and bolts, all under a 10 kg + 600 mm cube envelope.

parts.name: chassis_box_frame
parts.role: main structural box-frame carrying the drivetrain, scoop arm pickup, and bumper

parts.name: front_scoop_arm
parts.role: cantilevered arm that lifts both payload cubes at a 0.3 m moment arm forward of the front axle

parts.name: scoop_payload_fixture
parts.role: end-effector shape that captures the two 1.5 kg cubes on the arm tip

parts.name: drivetrain_cross_member
parts.role: cross-tube carrying axle mounts and transmitting wheel loads into the chassis box

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: bolts/nuts connecting chassis, arm, fixture, and cross-member

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: fits within 600 mm x 600 mm x 600 mm starting cube
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward, Y transverse, Z up; origin at chassis geometric center

load_cases.name: payload_lift
load_cases.application: front scoop arm at 0.3 m arm

boundary_conditions.interfaces: scoop arm bolted to chassis box at shoulder pivot; fixture bolted to arm tip; drivetrain cross-member bolted into chassis; fastener stiffness representative of commercial bolts
boundary_conditions.support: four wheel contact patches pinned during LC1-LC4

prompt.objective: minimize total assembly mass while meeting all pass/fail criteria

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.

interfaces.load_path: scoop shoulder pivot load into chassis
payload lift load from fixture into arm
wheel / step impulse into chassis through cross-member
fastener set transfers all inter-member loads

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The frame is explicitly described as tube + plate construction with a cantilevered scoop arm and end-effector bolted to the chassis box and a separate drivetrain cross-member; these are distinct structural parts.
notes.speculation_caveats: Part-family counts and interface types are speculative; materials and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
